name: Gratitude & Thankfulness
description: Quotes on appreciation, counting blessings, and the quiet power of a thankful heart.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: Gratitude is not only the greatest of virtues, but the parent of all the others.
quotes.notes: A common English rendering of Cicero's line on gratia from his defense oration Pro Plancio; wording varies by translation.

quotes.content: Gratitude makes sense of our past, brings peace for today, and creates a vision for tomorrow.
quotes.notes: From Beattie's daily meditation book on codependency and recovery.

quotes.content: I would maintain that thanks are the highest form of thought, and that gratitude is happiness doubled by wonder.
quotes.notes: From Chapter 6 of Chesterton's history, on the medieval spirit of thankfulness.

quotes.content: O Lord that lends me life, lend me a heart replete with thankfulness!
quotes.notes: Spoken by King Henry on being reunited with Queen Margaret.

quotes.content: We can only be said to be alive in those moments when our hearts are conscious of our treasures.
quotes.notes: From Wilder's novel reflecting on how gratitude for what we have gives life its meaning.

quotes.content: Do not spoil what you have by desiring what you have not; remember that what you now have was once among the things you only hoped for.
quotes.source: Quoted in Seneca, Letters to Lucilius, IX (c. 65 CE)
quotes.notes: Epicurus's counsel on contentment, preserved by Seneca in his ninth moral letter.

quotes.content: As we express our gratitude, we must never forget that the highest appreciation is not to utter words, but to live by them.
quotes.notes: From one of Kennedy's final proclamations, issued weeks before his assassination.

quotes.content: Feeling gratitude and not expressing it is like wrapping a present and not giving it.
quotes.notes: From the American inspirational writer known for his aphorisms on thankfulness.

quotes.content: He is a wise man who does not grieve for the things which he has not, but rejoices for those which he has.
quotes.notes: A Stoic maxim traditionally attributed to Epictetus on being content with what one possesses.

quotes.content: Be thankful for what you have; you'll end up having more. If you concentrate on what you don't have, you will never, ever have enough.
quotes.notes: A frequently cited reflection from Winfrey on the abundance mindset of gratitude.

quotes.content: Gratitude is the fairest blossom which springs from the soul.
quotes.notes: From a collection of sayings gathered from Beecher's sermons at Plymouth Church.

quotes.content: If the only prayer you ever say in your entire life is thank you, it will be enough.
quotes.notes: Widely attributed to the medieval mystic, though it does not appear verbatim in his surviving sermons.

quotes.content: Let us be grateful to the people who make us happy; they are the charming gardeners who make our souls blossom.
quotes.notes: Long attributed to Proust in translation; the exact French source is uncertain.

quotes.content: Reflect upon your present blessings, of which every man has many; not on your past misfortunes, of which all men have some.
quotes.notes: From Dickens's early collection of sketches, in the piece 'A Christmas Dinner.'

quotes.content: At times our own light goes out and is rekindled by a spark from another person. Each of us has cause to think with deep gratitude of those who have lighted the flame within us.
quotes.notes: From the physician and Nobel laureate's reflections on the people who shaped him.

quotes.content: Let gratitude be the pillow upon which you kneel to say your nightly prayer.
quotes.notes: Often cited from Angelou's talks and interviews on faith and thankfulness.

quotes.content: Gratitude is the healthiest of all human emotions. The more you express gratitude for what you have, the more likely you will have even more to express gratitude for.
quotes.notes: From the motivational speaker and author's talks on a positive mental attitude.

quotes.content: Gratitude turns what we have into enough.
quotes.notes: A widely circulated proverb of uncertain origin, sometimes expanded to 'and more.'

quotes.content: Silent gratitude isn't much use to anyone.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the English novelist Gladys Bronwyn Stern.

quotes.content: Gratitude goes beyond the 'mine' and 'thine' and claims the truth that all of life is a pure gift.
quotes.notes: From the Dutch priest and writer's reflections on the spiritual life of thankfulness.

quotes.content: When I started counting my blessings, my whole life turned around.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the musician, echoing the theme of his song 'Count Your Blessings.'

quotes.content: 'Thank you' is the best prayer that anyone could say. I say that one a lot. Thank you expresses extreme gratitude, humility, understanding.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the author of The Color Purple in interviews on gratitude.

quotes.content: For each new morning with its light, for rest and shelter of the night, for health and food, for love and friends, for everything Thy goodness sends.
quotes.notes: A verse of thanks widely attributed to Emerson; the attribution is traditional.

quotes.content: We often take for granted the very things that most deserve our gratitude.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the American essayist and novelist.

quotes.content: When we focus on our gratitude, the tide of disappointment goes out and the tide of love rushes in.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the writer and fitness columnist Kristin Armstrong.

quotes.content: Gratitude is the sign of noble souls.
quotes.notes: A moral traditionally drawn from Aesop's fables; wording varies by translation.

quotes.content: Happiness cannot be traveled to, owned, earned, worn or consumed. Happiness is the spiritual experience of living every minute with love, grace, and gratitude.
quotes.notes: From the motivational author and speaker on the psychology of winning.

quotes.content: Nothing is more honorable than a grateful heart.
quotes.notes: A maxim drawn from Seneca's treatise 'On Benefits,' his extended study of giving and gratitude.

quotes.content: Wear gratitude like a cloak, and it will feed every corner of your life.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the Persian poet and Sufi mystic; a popular rendering rather than a fixed translation.

quotes.content: Let us rise up and be thankful, for if we didn't learn a lot today, at least we learned a little, and if we didn't learn a little, at least we didn't get sick, and if we got sick, at least we didn't die; so, let us all be thankful.
quotes.notes: Popularly attributed to the Buddha; the source is a modern paraphrase rather than a canonical text.
